app.name: SafeTool Sync
app.description: Mirror synchronization between disks and folders

setup.title: Configure synchronization
setup.precompute_hashes_desc: Computes SHA-256 of every file before comparison. Required to detect conflicts where name, size and modification date match but content differs. Slower.
setup.source_not_found: The source folder does not exist.
setup.create_dest_message:
The destination folder does not exist. Create it?
{path}
setup.preset_custom_hint: Adjust the advanced parameters to your needs
setup.analyze_disabled_tooltip: Select source and destination folders to start

presets.mirror_exact_desc: Exact replica of source. Destination-only files are permanently deleted.
presets.mirror_exact_detail: Creates an identical copy of the source. Files existing only in the destination are permanently deleted (no trash). Ideal for backups that must be exact replicas.
presets.mirror_safe_desc: Safe replica. Destination-only files are sent to trash.
presets.mirror_safe_detail: Creates a copy of the source but files that are only in the destination go to the trash instead of being deleted. Recommended option for most cases.
presets.copy_only_desc: Only copies new and modified files. Does not delete anything from destination.
presets.copy_only_detail: Only adds new files and updates modified ones. Does not touch or delete anything from the destination. Fast comparison without hashes. Ideal for adding content without risk.
presets.mirror_hash_desc: Mirror with full SHA-256 hash. Detects any content difference.
presets.mirror_hash_detail: Computes SHA-256 hash of all files to detect any content difference, even when size and date match. Slower but maximum reliability. Destination-only files go to trash.
presets.two_way_safe_desc: Sync both sides. Conflicts are resolved manually.
presets.two_way_safe_detail: Copies new files from source to destination and vice versa. Files modified on both sides generate conflicts resolved step by step. Does not delete files from either side.
presets.two_way_exact_desc: Sync both sides. Source wins on conflicts.
presets.two_way_exact_detail: Copies new files in both directions. Files modified on both sides are resolved using the source version. Does not delete files from either side.
presets.two_way_hash_desc: Bidirectional with full SHA-256 hash. Maximum conflict detection.
presets.two_way_hash_detail: Computes SHA-256 hash of all files to detect any content difference in both directions. Conflicts are marked for manual resolution. Slower but no change goes undetected.
presets.custom_detail: Allows adjusting each parameter: direction, comparison mode, conflict policy, verification and trash. The advanced panel below is unlocked.

directions.unidirectional_desc: Syncs from source to destination. Source is the authority.
directions.bidirectional_desc: Syncs in both directions. New files are copied both ways and conflicts require a decision.

compare_modes.fast_desc: Compare by size and modification date
compare_modes.smart_desc: Fast + SHA-256 hash only on discrepancies

verify_modes.full_desc: Verify SHA-256 hash of each file after copying

diff_types_help.title: File statuses
diff_types_help.intro: Each compared file receives a status based on the differences found between source and destination.
diff_types_help.identical_desc: The file exists on both sides and is exactly the same (same size, date and content). No action will be taken.
diff_types_help.source_only_desc: The file exists only in the source. It has no copy in the destination. By default it will be copied to the destination.
diff_types_help.dest_only_desc: The file exists only in the destination. It has no copy in the source. It can be deleted, sent to trash, or copied to the source.
diff_types_help.modified_desc: The file exists on both sides but differs in size, date or content. The source is newer or different.
diff_types_help.conflict_pending_desc: Both sides have different versions of the file and no decision has been made on which prevails. Requires manual resolution before executing.
diff_types_help.conflict_auto_desc: The conflict has been resolved: a specific action has been assigned (overwrite source, overwrite destination, keep, etc.).
diff_types_help.renamed_desc: The file was renamed or moved. Detected by content match (SHA-256 hash). It will be renamed in the destination instead of copy and delete.
diff_types_help.error_source_desc: The file in the source could not be read (permissions, damaged disk, etc.). It will be skipped or marked for review.
diff_types_help.error_dest_desc: The file in the destination could not be read (permissions, damaged disk, etc.). It will be skipped or marked for review.

info.compare_mode_text:
• Fast: Compare by size and modification date. Does not detect changes if size and date match.
• Smart: Same as Fast, but computes SHA-256 hash when discrepancies exist or precomputed hashes are available.
• Full Hash: Computes and compares SHA-256 hash of all files. Detects any content difference.
info.precompute_hashes_text:
Computes the SHA-256 hash of every file (source and destination) before starting the comparison.

Useful to detect invisible conflicts where name, size and modification date are identical but content differs — situations Fast and Smart modes cannot detect without precomputed hashes.

Enabled automatically when Full Hash mode is selected. Increases analysis time proportionally to data volume.
info.conflict_policy_text:
Defines what to do when a file was modified on both sides (conflict):
• Overwrite destination: Source always wins.
• Keep destination: Keeps the destination version.
• Overwrite source: Destination wins (bidirectional only).
• Keep source: Keeps the source version (bidirectional only).
• Skip: Does not touch the file.
• Leave pending: Marks the conflict to resolve it in the review screen.
info.dest_only_action_text:
Defines what to do with files that exist only in the destination (no copy in source):
• Leave untouched: Keeps them as-is. Ideal for 'Copy Only'.
• Move to trash: Sends them to the system trash.
• Delete permanently: Removes them without recovery.
• Copy to source: Copies them to the source (bidirectional only).
info.direction_text:
• Unidirectional (mirror): Changes go from source to destination. Source is always the authority.
• Bidirectional (two-way): New files are copied in both directions. Files modified on both sides generate conflicts you must resolve.
info.verify_mode_text:
• Off: No post-copy verification.
• Spot Check: Verify a random percentage of copied files.
• Full: Verify SHA-256 hash of each file after copying.
info.use_trash_text: If enabled, files deleted from the destination will be sent to the system trash instead of being permanently deleted.
info.exc_system_files:
Excludes hidden files created by the operating system:
• .DS_Store (macOS)
• Thumbs.db (Windows)
• desktop.ini (Windows)
info.exc_dev_folders:
Excludes folders generated by development tools:
• .git (Repositories)
• node_modules (Node.js)
• __pycache__, .venv, .tox (Python)
info.exc_temp_files:
Excludes temporary or backup files:
• *.tmp, *.bak
• ~* (Temporary files)
• *.swp (Swap files)
info.custom_exclusions_text:
Enter comma-separated patterns to exclude specific files or folders.

Examples:
• *.log (all log files)
• *.bak (backup files)
• temp/ (temp folder and its contents)
• secret.txt (specific file)

actions_help.title: Sync Actions Guide
actions_help.intro: Detailed explanation of each possible action that can be applied to the analyzed files:
actions_help.copy_to_dest_desc: The file exists only in the source. It will be copied to the destination to keep both directories synchronized.
actions_help.copy_to_source_desc: The file exists only in the destination (bidirectional mode). It will be copied to the source so both sides are synchronized.
actions_help.overwrite_dest_desc: The file is different or newer in the source. The destination file will be replaced with the source version.
actions_help.overwrite_source_desc: The file is different or newer in the destination (bidirectional mode). The source file will be replaced with the destination version.
actions_help.delete_from_dest_desc: The file exists only in the destination and not in the source. In exact mirror mode, it will be permanently deleted from the destination.
actions_help.move_to_trash_desc: The file exists only in the destination. Instead of being permanently deleted, it will be sent to the system trash for safety.
actions_help.rename_in_dest_desc: The file was detected as renamed or moved in the source. It will be renamed directly in the destination to avoid deleting and re-copying it, saving time and space.
actions_help.keep_dest_desc: Keeps the current version of the file in the destination, ignoring any changes from the source. The source file is not touched.
actions_help.keep_source_desc: Keeps the current version of the file in the source, ignoring any changes from the destination (bidirectional mode). The destination file is not touched.
actions_help.skip_desc: Performs no operation on this file. Both versions remain untouched as they are.
actions_help.mark_review_desc: The file requires manual attention or decision. It will be skipped during execution and left pending for you to review later.

smart_match.title: Smart Comparison
smart_match.button_tooltip: Find matches between source-only and dest-only files by name and size
smart_match.description: Finds files that are likely the same but with slightly different names (suffixes, versions, copies). Matches by exact size and common name prefix.
smart_match.all_matched: All orphan files have a match
